title: eremostachys laciniata subsp. iberica
intro: Eremostachys laciniata subsp. iberica, also known as the Iberian mountain knotgrass or Iberian desert knotgrass, is a subspecies of the Eremostachys laciniata plant, a member of the mint family (Lamiaceae). This plant is traditionally used in folk medicine in various regions, particularly in the Middle East and Asia, due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Eremostachys laciniata subsp. iberica include its potential to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its ability to reduce fever and alleviate digestive problems. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, where it is combined with other herbs to create remedies for various ailments.
